player_notes.austin reaves: Game-time decision for Lakers in Game 5 vs. Rockets after ~4 weeks out with a Grade 2 oblique strain. Team is optimistic about his return; monitor pre-game reports before betting his props.
player_notes.jalen brunson: Media narrative pushing for more KAT usage in Knicks offense; potential shift in usage/shot volume away from Brunson if coaching staff adjusts.
player_notes.joel embiid: Returned for 76ers vs. Celtics but his presence was debated as potentially harmful; monitor minutes and role if series continues.
player_notes.aaron gordon: Ruled out for Nuggets Game 5 (already played); if series continues, calf/hamstring issue is an ongoing absence to track for Game 6.

game_notes.LAL vs HOU: Austin Reaves is a game-time decision (returning from oblique strain); his availability will impact Lakers rotation and props for LeBron, AD, and Reaves himself. LeBron had a poor Game 4 (10 pts, 8 TO).
game_notes.MIN vs DEN: Aaron Gordon out for Nuggets with hamstring; Timberwolves also dealing with injuries to Donte DiVincenzo and Anthony Edwards — monitor availability for Game 6. Jokic posted a triple-double in Game 5 win.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Austin Reaves (LAL) — Grade 2 oblique strain (Apr 2 vs OKC, same game as Doncic). 4-6 week timeline. Per Charania (Apr 13): 'out for the majority of the first-round series; earliest first week of May.' 12-25% reinjury risk if rushed.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news reports (Shams Charania, ESPN) that Austin Reaves is a game-time decision for Lakers in Game 5 and that the Lakers are 'optimistic' for his return. This indicates he is now actively under consideration to play in Game 5, which is earlier than the 'first week of May' estimate and changes his status from a confirmed absence to a live game-time decision.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update Reaves' status from 'out for majority of first-round series' to 'game-time decision for Game 5 (Apr 28).' Remove the 'earliest first week of May' language. Note that his return would significantly change LAL's offensive profile and reduce LeBron's sole-engine burden.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Aaron Gordon (DEN) — Calf/hamstring strains recurring over past two seasons. Missed 40 games this season. DEN's key defensive anchor. If he misses games vs MIN, DEN defense suffers significantly.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news confirms 'Nuggets rule out Aaron Gordon for Game 5 with calf tightness' — the headline references 'left hamstring tightness' in the description. The context doc's G5 diary entry for DEN-MIN already notes Gordon was 'QUES pre-game' for G3 but does not definitively confirm his G5 status. The news item confirms he was officially ruled OUT for G5.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update the playoff injury landscape to reflect Gordon was officially ruled OUT for Game 5 vs MIN with calf/hamstring tightness. The G5 diary entry should be updated to note his confirmed absence. His availability for G6 is now the key monitoring variable.

suggested_context_updates.player_or_team: Cooper Flagg / Rookie of the Year
suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Stephon Castle (SAS): Won Rookie of the Year 2024-25. Now a full starter in year two.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news reports 'Cooper Flagg tops Kon Knueppel for ROY honors in tight race' — Flagg (DAL) won Rookie of the Year for the 2025-26 season. This is not a conflict with Castle's 2024-25 ROY award, but it confirms the 2025-26 ROY winner is Cooper Flagg, which is a new confirmed fact not present in the context document.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add a note that Cooper Flagg (DAL) won the 2025-26 Rookie of the Year award. This is new information not conflicting with Castle's 2024-25 ROY but fills a gap in the context document. Note: DAL is eliminated, so this has no playoff prop relevance.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Game 4 (Apr 27) — OKC 131, PHX 122 | OKC leads 4-0 — SERIES CLINCHED (margin 9) [listed under playoff series diary as the most recent entry]
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Today's news confirms OKC completed the sweep: 'Gilgeous-Alexander scores 31 and Thunder outlast Suns 131-122 to cap a 4-game sweep' and 'Thunder complete sweep of Suns to reach Western Conference semis.' The series result in the diary is accurate and consistent with the news. No conflict — confirming existing data.
